changes: Added clear red P1 and blue P2 identity tags during two-player expeditions.
Gave Player 2 a dedicated blue floating companion that follows them in town, the shop, and expeditions.
Added the Pokemon world with Pikachu, Charmander, Mystery Dungeon rooms, and a three-boss rotation.
Balanced item sprite sizes across the market, storage, menus, and shop displays; single-item stands retain a little extra presence.
Customers now pause their own activity while shop menus are open and walk to the display holding the item they want.
Added the secret encyclopedia review mode with checklists and Markdown export for sprite audits.
Standardized Adventurers Guild portraits around each hero's default idle pose and a consistent visual size.
Improved two-player menu clarity with matched viewport filtering, selectable UI sizes, larger Large-mode text, safe screen bounds, and F11 fullscreen.
Polished hero movement, dodges, attacks, combat effects, hitboxes, and Dragon Ball presentation across several playtest passes.
Added franchise-specific barrier art to each expedition world.
Made customer orders visible, expanded order capacity, and added an in-game shop handbook.
Reworked furniture attention so placement, attraction, and slot selection are easier to understand.
Expanded and corrected customer sprite pools, including Kanto and Final Fantasy visitors.
Added playable Goku and Piccolo with captured movement, flight, melee, and beam attacks.
Rebuilt Sora, Mario, Luigi, and Piccolo animations from live reference captures.
Brought more life to the Crossroads plaza with moving franchise travelers and readable name tags.
Added an expedition pause-and-retreat menu and improved Strike Raid's presentation.
Added autosaves at every portion of the day and made them directly loadable.
Gave both players independent expedition item belts and ensured every offered consumable has a working effect.
Captured and added Link's authentic Minish Cap walk, idle, sword, and dodge animations.
Improved world rooms with clamped cameras, safer spawns, cleaner barriers, and world-themed HUD elements.
Expanded Boom events into clearly announced customer crowds.
Audited game content so incomplete worlds and placeholder content are represented honestly.
Built the Final Fantasy world with Cloud, a full enemy roster, town rooms, and rotating bosses.
Built the Hyrule world with Link, sword attacks, bombs, Minish Cap enemies, and animated bosses.
Built the Hidden Leaf world with Naruto enemies, rooms, and Jump Ultimate customers.
Replaced stretched obstacle textures with clean individual props throughout expedition worlds.
Improved split-screen menu scaling and HUD placement.
Added local two-player split-screen with independent movement, menus, zoom, HUDs, and co-op expeditions.
Added full controller navigation, controller-aware prompts, right-stick scrolling, and trigger zoom.
Painted and expanded the shop with furniture progression, decor, visible stand-top items, and clearer recipes.
Added dozens of franchise items and customers while auditing the live catalog for usable sprite art.
Built the Mushroom Kingdom expedition and substantially expanded the Kingdom Hearts presentation.
Added Patch as the player's floating sidekick alongside a broad music, voice, and sound-effect pass.
Improved negotiations, customer buying-power feedback, Boom arrivals, and daily briefings.
Added the illustrated title screen with fullscreen-safe selection bars and ornate menus.
Reskinned story scenes with a starry backdrop, character portraits, Patch voice clips, and new music.
Built out the Crossroads plaza with supplied building art and an ornate HUD.
Added the furniture catalog, transition curtain, Final Fantasy customer pool, and more shop polish.
Restricted the circulating catalog to items with real sprite art while allowing franchises to mix naturally.
Completed the first playable game loop spanning town, shopkeeping, combat, story, saving, and tests.
Completed the Kingdom Hearts vertical slice and wired the first large batch of supplied character, world, and item art.
Added the game-wide ChronoType font, title artwork, music overrides, and repaired audio tracks.
Added endless-mode rent, economy hardening, export support, and long-run balance verification.
Added in-game developer and location-workshop tools for faster content building.
Created the Crossroads Godot project foundation, core data packs, autoload systems, and placeholder art.
